description:
## What's Wrong
Repo docs/config mention a coverage job and a `cargo xtask coverage` path, but the repo has no `xtask` crate and current workflows don't include the referenced coverage job.

## Where
- .github/AGENTS.md (mentions coverage + xtask)
- .cargo/config.toml (defines `xtask` alias)
- .github/workflows/ci.yml (current actual CI)

## Why It Matters
Stale CI documentation misleads contributors and future agents. The `xtask` alias suggests functionality that doesn't exist.

## Suggested Fix
Either:
1) Add an `xtask/` crate and wire a real coverage job, or
2) Remove the alias and update .github/AGENTS.md to match reality.

## Acceptance
- CI docs accurately describe the workflows that actually run.
- No dead `cargo xtask` alias unless backed by a crate.
title: CI docs drift: xtask/coverage references but no xtask crate

description:
**Problem** Gascity invokes \`bd init --server -p --skip-hooks [--server-host H] [--server-port P]\` (see gascity/internal/beads/bdstore.go:116-125). beads-rs's \`bd init\` does not recognize any of these flags and errors out on \`--server\`. **Design** Accept every flag gascity passes. Most are Dolt-specific and become no-ops in beads-rs: - \`--server\` — accept, ignore. No Dolt server in beads-rs. - \`--server-host \` — accept, ignore. - \`--server-port

\` — accept, ignore. - \`--skip-hooks\` — accept, honor (skip git hook installation if beads-rs does that; otherwise no-op). - \`-p/--prefix \` — accept, honor. Sets the issue-ID prefix for beads created in this repo (e.g., -p foo → IDs like \`foo-xyz\`). Print a single debug line naming which flags were ignored as Dolt-only. Do not warn loudly on every init. **Acceptance** - [ ] \`bd init --server -p myproj --skip-hooks --server-host x.y --server-port 1234\` succeeds - [ ] Resulting store has prefix \"myproj\" (verified via \`bd create \"test\" --json | jq -r .data.id\` → \`myproj-...\`) - [ ] \`bd init --help\` documents every flag (accept-and-ignore ones labeled as compat) - [ ] \`cargo xtest\` green **Depends on** Floor 0 init-no-remote-tolerance (same file). Can be worked sequentially or together. **Files likely touched** - \`crates/beads-cli/src/commands/init.rs\` - \`crates/beads-bootstrap/src/\` (prefix propagation)
title: Floor 0: bd init accept gascity's flag set as no-ops

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Targeted slow suites use reusable condition-based waits instead of repeated fixed sleeps
- [ ] Known readiness races have explicit barriers or polling predicates
- [ ] Test runtime drops measurably in the affected suites without reducing coverage
closed_reason: Condition-driven waits replaced the remaining fixed sleep/retry loops in the targeted slow suites.
description:
**Problem**
The slow suite still relies on many fixed sleeps and bespoke polling loops in `critical_path`, `migration`, `tailnet_proxy`, and `repl_rig`. Those sleeps add real wall time and create timing-sensitive flakes.

**Design**
Replace fixed-duration waiting with shared condition-based wait helpers and explicit readiness barriers. Keep timeouts bounded and observable, but stop paying one-second sleeps when the system is already ready.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/critical_path.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/repl_rig.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/tailnet_proxy.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/daemon/*.rs`

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
This bead has real progress but is not closed yet.

Landed so far:
- shared condition-based waits in `fixtures/wait.rs`
- adoption in `tailnet_proxy`, `critical_path`, `legacy_store`, `bd_runtime`, and crash/lifecycle helpers
- fresh post-restart handshake barrier for crash/restart tailnet tests
- default tailnet smoke profile made deterministic again so lossy frame faults only live in the explicit pathological tests

Remaining wait/retry duplication still open under this bead:
- repeated backoff loops inside `repl_rig`
- reconnect sleeps in `subscribe`
- local polling helper in `daemon/logging.rs`

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Shared condition-based wait helpers now cover the remaining slow-suite retry loops that were still open under this bead.

Landed in the current stack:
- `fixtures/wait.rs` now owns the reusable backoff/retry surface used by slow integration tests
- `fixtures/repl_rig.rs` moved `wait_for_show` and `wait_for_admin_ready` onto the shared polling helpers
- `daemon/subscribe.rs` moved reconnect sleeps onto `wait::retry_with_backoff(...)`
- `daemon/logging.rs` no longer owns its own local polling helper

Fresh verification receipts from `/Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs`:
- `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration daemon::repl_e2e::repl_daemon_to_daemon_roundtrip --features slow-tests -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration daemon::repl_e2e::repl_daemon_crash_restart_tailnet_roundtrip --features slow-tests -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration daemon::repl_e2e::repl_daemon_pathological_tailnet_roundtrip --features slow-tests -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration daemon::subscribe --features slow-tests -- --nocapture`
- `cargo xtest --no-fail-fast --status-level none --final-status-level slow`
- `cargo nextest run --profile slow --workspace --all-features --features slow-tests --no-fail-fast --status-level none --final-status-level slow`

title: Replace fixed sleeps with condition-driven wait helpers in slow tests
